title: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) - Rule 9.1 - Communication from the applicant (23/03/2026) in the case of Karsli and Others v. Türkiye (Application No. 18693/20) (Yuksel Yalcinkaya group) [anglais uniquement]
text: SECRETARIAT / SECRÉTARIAT SECRETARIAT OF THE COMMITTEE OF MINISTERS SECRÉTARIAT DU COMITÉ DES MINISTRES Contact: Ireneusz Kondak Tel: 03.90.21.59.86 Date: 24/03/2026 DH-DD(2026)384 Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. Meeting: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) Communication from the applicant (23/03/2026) in the case of Karsli and Others v. Türkiye (Application No. 18693/20) (Yuksel Yalcinkaya group) (appendices in Turkish are available at the Secretariat upon request) . Information made available under Rule 9.1 of the Rules of the Committee of Ministers for the supervision of the execution of judgments and of the terms of friendly settlements. * * * * * * * * * * * Document distribué sous la seule responsabilité de son auteur, sans préjuger de la position juridique ou politique du Comité des Ministres. Réunion : 1563e réunion (juin 2026) (DH) Communication du requérant (23/03/2026) relative à l’affaire Karsli et autres c. Türkiye (requête n° 18693/20) (groupe Yuksel Yalcinkaya) (des annexes en Turque sont disponibles auprès du Secrétariat sur demande). [anglais uniquement] Informations mises à disposition en vertu de la Règle 9.1 des Règles du Comité des Ministres pour la surveillance de l’exécution des arrêts et des termes des règlements amiables. DH-DD(2026)384: Communication from Türkiye. Documents distributed at the request of a Representative shall be under the sole responsibility of the said DGI Representative, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. 23 MARS 2026 SERVICE DE L’EXECUTION Dear Sir or Madam, DES ARRETS DE LA CEDH On 3 March 2026, I submitted information to the Committee of Ministers regarding a serious issue concerning the execution of the final judgment of the European Court of Human Rights in Duman v. Türkiye (Application no. 18583/22, joined to Karslı and Others v. Türkiye – 18693/20), delivered on 16 December 2025 and which became final on the same date. Following the finalisation of the judgment, an application for the reopening of the criminal proceedings, together with a request for the suspension of execution, was duly lodged before the Kırıkkale 1st Assize Court. However, by an additional decision dated 11 February 2026, the domestic court rejected the request for retrial and the suspension of execution, despite explicitly acknowledging the existence and finality of the ECtHR judgment. An objection was duly lodged against the above-mentioned decision. However, on 23 March 2026, the Kırıkkale 2nd Assize Court dismissed the objection, stating that there was no ECtHR judgment delivered specifically in the name of the applicant. This reasoning raises serious concerns as to whether the scope and content of the ECtHR judgment were properly assessed or even read by the domestic court. As a result, the refusal to reopen the criminal proceedings and to suspend execution has now become final. In these circumstances, I respectfully submit this information to the Committee of Ministers for its consideration within the framework of the supervision of the execution of judgments under Article 46 § 2 of the Convention, as the refusal to reopen the proceedings and to suspend execution appears to constitute a clear and continuing difficulty in execution. Should the Committee require any further documents or clarifications, I remain at its disposal. Yours faithfully, Av. Enes Malik Kılıç Attorney-at-law Counsel for the applicant Istanbul, Türkiye ANNEX: Final decision of the Kırıkkale 2nd Assize Court dated 23 March 2026 rejecting the request for reopening of the proceedings 1
case_name: CASE OF KARSLI AND OTHERS v. TÜRKİYE
